Class OpenAiModel

java.lang.Object
uno.anahata.asi.agi.provider.AbstractModel
uno.anahata.asi.openai.OpenAiModel
public class OpenAiModel extends AbstractModel
Native implementation for OpenAI models using the Responses API (/v1/responses).

This implementation performs "Partitioned Construction" of the request payload by assembling the full API body and then extracting the Identity (Config) and Memory (History) JSON strings from it for UI visibility. Supports built-in hosted tools like Web Search and Code Interpreter.

Author:
anahata

  • Constructor Details

    • OpenAiModel

      public OpenAiModel(OpenAiResponsesProvider provider, com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode node)
      Constructs a new OpenAiModel from an API model node.
      Parameters:
      provider - The parent provider.
      node - The JSON node containing model metadata.

  • Method Details

    • countTokens

      public int countTokens(String text)
      Counts the number of tokens in the given text string using this model's specific tokenizer.

      Utilizes TokenizerUtils to perform offline, BPE token counting based on configured TokenizerType.

      Specified by:
      countTokens in class AbstractModel
      Parameters:
      text - The text to count tokens for.
      Returns:
      The token count, or 0 if the text is null or empty.

    • countTokens

      public int countTokens(AbstractToolCall<?,?> toolCall)
      Counts the number of tokens consumed by the given tool call in the context window.

      Model subclasses override this method to serialize the tool call into its exact wire-format (JSON, Protobuf, etc.) to ensure 100% accurate billing.

      Serializes the tool call into a standard OpenAI function call JSON object containing 'name' and 'arguments' properties, and counts its tokens using the active BPE encoding.

      Specified by:
      countTokens in class AbstractModel
      Parameters:
      toolCall - The tool call to count tokens for.
      Returns:
      The total token count.

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Gets a detailed description of the model's capabilities and limitations.

      Implementation details: Returns the display name or model ID.

      Specified by:
      getDescription in class AbstractModel
      Returns:
      The model description.

    • getVersion

      public String getVersion()
      Gets the version string for this model.

      Implementation details: Always returns null as Responses API models use the base ID for versioning.

      Specified by:
      getVersion in class AbstractModel
      Returns:
      The version.

      public int getMaxInputTokens()
      Gets the maximum number of input tokens supported by this model.
      Specified by:
      getMaxInputTokens in class AbstractModel
      Returns:
      The input token limit.

      Gets the maximum number of output tokens this model can generate in a single turn.

      Gets the list of supported API actions for this model (e.g., "generateContent").
      Specified by:

      Returns:
      A list of supported actions.

    • getRawDescription

      public String getRawDescription()
      Gets a rich, potentially HTML-formatted description of the model, including all its metadata.

      Returns a high-density HTML summary of the model and its specialized Responses API provider.

      Specified by:
      getRawDescription in class AbstractModel
      Returns:
      The raw description string.

      Returns:

    • getAvailableServerTools

      public List<ServerTool> getAvailableServerTools()
      Gets the list of server-side tools available for this model.

      Implementation details: Provides 'web_search' and 'code_interpreter' as native server-side tools.

      Specified by:
      getAvailableServerTools in class AbstractModel
      Returns:
      A list of available server tools.

    • getDefaultServerTools

      public List<ServerTool> getDefaultServerTools()
      Gets the list of server-side tools that should be enabled by default for this model.
      Specified by:
      getDefaultServerTools in class AbstractModel
      Returns:
      The list of default server tools.

    • getDefaultTemperature

      public Float getDefaultTemperature()
      Gets the default temperature for this model.

      Temperature controls the randomness of the response. Higher values (e.g., 1.0) make the output more random, while lower values (e.g., 0.2) make it more deterministic.

      Specified by:
      getDefaultTemperature in class AbstractModel
      Returns:
      The default temperature, or null if not specified.

    • getDefaultTopK

      public Integer getDefaultTopK()
      Gets the default topK for this model.

      Top-K sampling limits the model's choices to the K most likely next tokens.

      Specified by:
      getDefaultTopK in class AbstractModel
      Returns:
      The default topK, or null if not specified.

    • getDefaultTopP

      public Float getDefaultTopP()
      Gets the default topP for this model.

      Top-P (nucleus) sampling selects tokens whose cumulative probability adds up to the threshold P.

      Specified by:
      getDefaultTopP in class AbstractModel
      Returns:
      The default topP, or null if not specified.

    • preparePayload

      private OpenAiModel.PreparedPayload preparePayload(GenerationRequest request, boolean stream)
      Assembles the full OpenAI Responses API payload and extracts partitions for UI visibility.
      Parameters:
      request - The generation request.
      stream - Whether to enable SSE streaming.
      Returns:
      The prepared payload partitions.

    • generateContent

      public Response generateContent(GenerationRequest request)
      The core method for interacting with an AI model. It takes a configuration object and a list of messages and returns a standardized Response.

      Executes a synchronous content generation request using the Responses API. Performs automated partitioning of the request for clear history and configuration separation in the UI.

      Specified by:
      generateContent in class AbstractModel
      Parameters:
      request - The generation request containing config and history.
      Returns:
      A standardized Response object.

    • generateContentStream

      public void generateContentStream(GenerationRequest request, StreamObserver<Response<? extends AbstractModelMessage>> observer)
      Generates content asynchronously using token streaming.

      Executes an asynchronous content generation request using the Responses API with Server-Sent Events (SSE). Streams text and reasoning in real-time, and seamlessly processes tool calls upon item completion.

      Specified by:
      generateContentStream in class AbstractModel
      Parameters:
      request - The generation request containing config and history.
      observer - The observer that will receive the streaming response chunks.

    • getToolDeclarationJson

      public String getToolDeclarationJson(AbstractTool<?,?> tool, RequestConfig config)
      Gets the provider-specific JSON representation of a tool's declaration. This is used by the UI to show exactly what is being sent to the model.

      Translates Anahata tool definitions into the OpenAI 'function' specification format.

      Specified by:
      getToolDeclarationJson in class AbstractModel
      Parameters:
      tool - The tool to inspect.
      config - The request configuration (e.g. to check useNativeSchemas).
      Returns:
      The JSON string representing the tool declaration.

    • countTokens

      public int countTokens(AbstractToolResponse<?> toolResponse)
      Counts the number of tokens consumed by the given tool execution response.

      Model subclasses override this to serialize the response into its exact wire-format (Protobuf FunctionResponse, JSON, etc.) to ensure 100% accurate billing.

      Serializes the base tool response to its flat JSON representation and adds the token cost of any generated attachments (like images or logs) by delegating to the generic binary tokenizer. This mirrors the exact OpenAI Responses API wire-format, where attachments are sent as a separate developer-role message.

      Specified by:
      countTokens in class AbstractModel
      Parameters:
      toolResponse - The tool response instance to count.
      Returns:
      The precise, billing-identical token count.

    • countTokens

      public int countTokens(byte[] data, String mimeType)
      Counts the number of tokens consumed by raw binary data based on its MIME type and model-specific multimodal billing rules.

      This generic signature provides complete decoupling from domain part classes, allowing the model to tokenize any binary payload (such as blob parts or tool attachments).

      Calculates the exact, model-specific multimodal token count for OpenAI image data. Delegates the header-only image dimension reading to the core ImageMetadataUtils utility, and performs the OpenAI-specific high-detail scaling and tiling calculations.

      Specified by:
      countTokens in class AbstractModel
      Parameters:
      data - The raw binary data of the file or attachment.
      mimeType - The detected MIME type of the binary data (e.g. "image/png").
      Returns:
      The precise, billing-identical multimodal token count.
